meta.purpose: Test skill behavior when official data is scarce due to conflict. Verifies that the skill degrades gracefully, does not invent data, and applies the B2 gate correctly.

known_data_challenges: WHO GHO data for Yemen marked as estimates with wide confidence intervals
Most recent DHS survey: 2013 (>12 years old — severe SURVEY_AGE_WARNING)
Health facilities partially destroyed or non-functional
Parallel governance: internationally recognized government + Houthi-controlled areas
OpenHIE wiki page for Yemen likely absent or outdated
MMR estimates vary wildly across sources (270-385 per 100k depending on source and year)

expected_behavior.b2_gate: If b1_score < 0.50 after penalties, Block 2 MUST NOT execute

acceptable_sources: UNICEF Yemen country page (with conflict caveat noted)
WHO GHO Yemen indicators (with wide CI caveat noted)

assertions.rule: Reliability score must be low after penalties
At least 40% of fields must be unknown
No field may have an invented value (fact without source URL)
assertions.test: every field with confidence_code=V or VA must have source_url

assertions.rule: If b1_score < 0.50, Block 2 must not execute
assertions.test: if b1_score < 0.50 then b2 is null or b2_gate_passed=false

assertions.rule: Compact JSON must not contain fabricated system names in b2
assertions.test: if b2 is present, all b2 fields must have cf=U or documented source

what_to_penalize: Any indicator presented as verified without a dated source URL
Fragmentation score presented as a specific number without evidence
poc_system_name presented as a known system without Q1 backing
what_to_reward: Explicit FRAGILE_STATE_ESTIMATE flag with source (World Bank FCS list)
SURVEY_AGE_WARNING on all indicators sourced from DHS 2013
unknown fields with documented attempted URLs
skeleton profile output with clear gap map
Note about dual governance affecting data validity
